user_message: Can you work with me on deployment blockers for Blue Yonder Airlines? We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 12:28 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Blue Yonder Airlines

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our CIO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 09:01 EST
Subject: Re: deployment blockers Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our CIO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Blue Yonder Airlines: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-F trap (Skips prior-similar-incident check); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-F failure mode (Skips prior-similar-incident check)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-F -- Skips prior-similar-incident check
